adds.rationale: Deepens AI-wave exposure beyond a single mega-cap (NVDA) into the broader semiconductor supply chain (TSMC, ASML, AMAT) that the entire AI buildout depends on. TSMC's Q1-2021 results flagged >100% fab utilization, raised 2021 capex to ~$30B, and committed $100B over three years to expand capacity — a multi-year picks-and-shovels tailwind for SMH's top holdings.

Cleanest US-listed liquid vehicle covering two next-wave thesis buckets simultaneously: robotics/autonomy (Tesla, Trimble, Kratos, industrial automation) and early rockets/spacecraft (Iridium, satellite-comms, aerospace primes). SpaceX Starship SN10 stuck its landing on 2021-03-03, validating the reusable-launch thesis at a moment when no pure-play public spacecraft ticker exists yet (RKLB is still private).

Captures the nuclear next-wave through uranium miners (Cameco, Kazatomprom, NexGen). Cameco's Q4-2020 release (Feb 9, 2021) flagged that ~two-thirds of utility uranium requirements will be uncovered by 2030, while the new US administration recommitted to maintaining the existing reactor fleet and supporting advanced reactor construction. Tight long-term supply plus net-zero policy alignment is a textbook 'invest early while cheap' setup.

rationale_overall: Watchlist is heavy AI/mega-cap-tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) plus SPY. With 11 slots free under the 16-cap, this round deepens AI breadth via SMH (semiconductor supply chain) and seeds two next-wave thesis buckets while still cheap: ARKQ for robotics + early rockets/spacecraft exposure (SpaceX SN10 just landed; no pure-play public rocket ticker exists yet), and URA for the nuclear wave (uranium supply gap by 2030, supportive US policy). No removes — current watchlist members are all core AI-wave names that the thesis says to ride, not trim, in early 2021.
